text: America's financial institutions are under pressure.
Every 22 seconds, another American becomes a victim of fraud and identity theft,
part of a global system that launders up to $2 trillion a year towards America's adversaries.
Today we announced we raised $21 million to build a new standard for identity investigations
and enhanced due diligence. Financial crime investigators are the last line of defense.
They have to navigate hundreds of counter parties, nested shell company structures,
and years of transactions and relationships using tools that were never designed for a post-AI world,
even as fraud grows faster, more complex, and better at exploiting gaps and controls.
Every risk team will eventually need to move from manual casework to agentic investigations.
Over the past two years, our AI agents have run investigations across the deepest parts of the
internet for some of the largest companies in the world. Through that process, we build the graph,
a system that lets AI agents conduct investigations in a structured, auditable way.
Here's what that looks like. A high-risk customer is flagged for review.
Variance is able to automatically trace the owner of that account through adverse media,
web lookups, and relationships with other customers through the graph.
Everything our agents find lives on the graph. People, businesses, transactions, relationships.
Every claim is backed by a query with an audit-ready map of evidence supporting each conclusion.
Our tech has already deployed at scale across multiple Fortune 500s, running hundreds of
thousands of investigations a day across risk compliance and enhanced due diligence.
Our agents are 10 times more accurate than conventional models and 100 times faster than
manual review. This is the transformation AI agents make possible. Investigations no longer
end when a case is closed. They add to a growing body of context and evidence that makes it harder
to exploit America's financial system. If you're a risk team looking to scale faster,
we would love to talk. And if you're excited by solving real,
hard, and adversarial problems, join the team.
